revision.text:
[[ja:箱崎八幡神社 (壱岐市)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine | native_name = 箱崎八幡神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.825023|129.738097}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Hachiman|lt=Hachiman|ar|هاتشيمان|az|Haçiman|ca|Hachiman|de|Hachiman|el|Χατσίμαν|en|Hachiman|es|Hachiman|eu|Hachiman|fa|هاچیمان|fi|Hachiman|fr|Hachiman|hu|Hacsiman|id|Hachiman|it|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|ko|하치만 신|mni|ꯍꯆꯤꯃꯥꯟ|my|ဟချိမန်း|pl|Hachiman|pt|Hachiman|ru|Хатиман|simple|Hachiman|sl|Hačiman|sv|Hachiman|tr|Hachiman|uk|Хачіман|uz|Hachiman|vi|Hachiman|wuu|八幡神|zh|八幡神|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine'''|箱崎八幡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iki Island]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q9610964|ltq=Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q11390939|ltq=Q11390939|qid=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Iki Island|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Iki Island|ja|壱岐島の式内社一覧|qid=Q11429584|1=List of Shikinaisha in Iki Island}} ** P155: {{ill|Q135041491|ltq=Q135041491|qid=Q135041491|1=Hyōzu Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135041494|ltq=Q135041494|qid=Q135041494|1=Kunikatanushino Shrine}} ** Series ordinal: 5 == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|lt=Iki|ar|إكي (ناغاساكي)|azb|ایکی، ناقاساکی|ceb|Iki Shi|ce|Ики (гӀала)|cs|Iki (Nagasaki)|de|Iki (Nagasaki)|en|Iki, Nagasaki|es|Iki (Nagasaki)|et|Iki|fa|ایکی، ناگاساکی|fi|Iki (kaupunki)|fr|Iki (Nagasaki)|he|איקי|hy|Իկի (քաղաք)|it|Iki (Nagasaki)|ja|壱岐市|ko|이키시|lt|Ikis (miestas)|mzn|ایکی، ناگازاکی|nl|Iki|pl|Iki (miasto)|pt|Iki (Nagasaki)|ro|Iki|ru|Ики (город)|sv|Iki, Nagasaki|tg|Ики|tl|Iki, Nagasaki|tr|Iki (şehir)|tt|Ики (шәһәр)|uk|Ікі (Нагасакі)|vi|Iki, Nagasaki|war|Iki, Nagasaki|zh_classical|壹岐市|zh_min_nan|Iki-chhī|zh_yue|壹岐|zh|壹岐市|qid=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}} * {{ill|Q494914|ltq=Q494914|qid=Q494914|1=Iki Island}} * {{ill|Q10931631|ltq=Q10931631|qid=Q10931631|1=Iki district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|33.825023|129.738097}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q261637|ltq=Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20160306025317/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/670104.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183339 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Tsukuyomi Shrine|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine|qid=Q135041492|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町箱崎釘の尾触823', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町箱崎釘ノ尾触', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:箱崎八幡神社 (壱岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q111442063}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine |Image = {{Image requested|cat=Iki City}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|qid=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki City|Q861519|qid=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}{{ill|lt=Ashibe Town|Q11614924|qid=Q11614924|1=Ashibe}} 823 Kugino-o-fure, Hakozaki |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Watatsumi|Q1474187|qid=Q1474187|lt=Toyotamabiko no Mikoto|1=Watatsumi}}, {{ill|lt=Tamayori-hime|Q56344982|qid=Q56344982|lt=Tamayori-hime no Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Nakatsuhime no Mikoto|Q11378496|qid=Q11378496|lt=Nakahiuri no Mikoto|1=Nakatsuhime}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Amatsuki no Kami no Mikoto|Q133796594|qid=Q133796594|1=Ame no Tsuki no Mitama no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Takami-Musubi|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi no Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (claimed), former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Hakozaki
Hachiman Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine''' (はこざきはちまんじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ashibe Town|Q11614924|qid=Q11614924|1=Ashibe}}, {{ill|lt=Iki City|Q861519|qid=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|qid=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. === Overview === It is identified with the Tsukuyomi Shrine and Takamimioya Shrine recorded in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Although there are other shrines identified as Tsukuyomi Shrine or Takamimioya Shrine, those assessments are said to be errors made by {{ill|lt=Tachibana no Miki|Q11544184|qid=Q11544184|1=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi}} during the {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|qid=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} era. Later, {{ill|lt=Hakozaki Shrine|Q714742|qid=Q714742|1=Hakozaki Shrine}} was transferred (kanjō), and the shrine came to be called Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine.Ashibe Town History Editorial Committee, *History of Ashibe Town* (Ashibe Town, 1978). The *Iki Shrine Records* state: “It was first founded as the shikinaisha Tsukuyomi Shrine; next, through the joint enshrinement of sea deities or dragon deities, it became a Dragon God or Undersea Palace shrine; later, with the additional enshrinement of the Hachiman deity, it came entirely under the shrine name Hachiman-gū, eclipsing the other shrine names. Therefore, in the appraisal of Enpō 4, it was mistakenly placed outside the shikinaisha.” According to documents handed down in the Yoshino family, descendants of the {{ill|lt=Iki no Agatanushi|UNKNOWN|lt=Iki clan}}, it was first enshrined on Mount Odake either on September 13 of {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|qid=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=724|Q23549|qid=Q23549|1=724}}) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Shōmu|Q349263|qid=Q349263|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, or in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|qid=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 6 ({{ill|lt=787|Q30327|qid=Q30327|1=AD 787}}).{{efn2|Regarding the reason for enshrinement: in the former tradition, it is said that the sea deity descended upon Hiso-yama; in the latter, that western invaders attacked and an imperial command was issued.}} Thereafter, it was successively transferred to Higashiyashiki in Kamisato, Tsuji in Shimosato, and Mount Miyaji in Shinjo Village, finally settling in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1700|Q6813|qid=Q6813|1=1700}}) at Mount Konedeyama in present-day Hakozaki Village. An inscription on a ridge tag of the inner sanctuary reads: “Hakozaki Hachiman-gū, Iki Province, Iki District, Tsukuyomi Shrine, Tsurube Estate, Shioyasu District; construction of one inner sanctuary building, by {{ill|lt=Dazaifu|Q144845|qid=Q144845|lt=Dazai no Daini|1=Dazaifu (government)}} concurrently {{ill|lt=Chikugo Province|Q864159|qid=Q864159|lt=Governor of Chikugo|1=Chikugo Province}}, {{ill|lt=Shōni Kagesuke|Q10263912|qid=Q10263912|lt=Fujiwara no Kagesuke|1=Shōni Kagesuke}},” with “{{ill|lt=Shōkyō|Q1069086|qid=Q1069086|1=Shōkyō}} 1” written to the right, and “Year Jinshin, 8th month, 5th day; Ōmiya-ji, Igi Suemochi” written to the left. The Tsukuyomi Shrine here does not enshrine {{ill|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|qid=Q595520|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi no Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} of general {{ill|lt=Japanese mythology|Q814158|qid=Q814158|1=Japanese mythology}}, but rather the Iki-specific deity {{ill|lt=Amatsuki no Kami no Mikoto|Q133796594|qid=Q133796594|1=Ame no Tsuki no Mitama no Mikoto}}. In the {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}, in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kensō|Q360966|qid=Q360966|1=Emperor Kenzō}}, a “moon god” possessed {{ill|lt=Abe no Kotodai|Q57538465|qid=Q57538465|1=Abe no Kotodai}}, an envoy to {{ill|lt=Mimana|Q1790223|qid=Q1790223|1=Mimana}}, declaring: “My ancestor {{ill|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi no Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} had the merit of creating heaven and earth when they were molten together. Offer me lands for the people. If you present them as I request, there will be blessings.” When Abe no Kotodai returned to the capital and reported this in detail to the emperor, Uta-arasuda in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kadono District, Yamashiro Province|1=UNKNOWN}} was granted to the moon god, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oshimi no Sukune|1=UNKNOWN}}, ancestor of the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iki no Agatanushi|1=UNKNOWN}}, performed the rites. The “moon god” appearing in this story is considered to be Amatsuki no Kami no Mikoto. “Uta-arasuda” is today the {{ill|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine (Kyoto City)|Q11516217|qid=Q11516217|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}, an auxiliary shrine outside the precincts of {{ill|lt=Matsunoo Taisha|Q710302|qid=Q710302|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Nishikyō Ward|Q1154588|qid=Q1154588|1=Nishikyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto City|Q34600|qid=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}, and is thought to have been divided from the Tsukuyomi Shrine of Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine.Katsutaka Hōjō, “On the Enshrinement of Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto at Matsunoo Taisha,” *Bulletin of the National Museum of Japanese History*, No. 72, March 1997. The Takamimioya Shrine enshrines {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi no Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}, who appears in the above account. It is said that {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mebarasuwari Takamimusubi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|qid=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}, was divided from the Takamimioya Shrine. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Primary sources === {{reflist|group="Primary"|2}} === References === {{reflist|2}} {{Shinto sidebar}} {{Authority control}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]]
